task_completion.description: Verify an active disease record for occupational noise exposure (ICD-10 Z57.0) was added for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a patient record, conditions list, or disease episode screen for 'John Zenon' showing 'Z57.0' or 'occupational noise exposure' as an active condition.

task_completion.description: Verify an active disease record for bilateral sensorineural hearing loss (ICD-10 H90.3 or any H90.x subcode) was added for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a patient record, conditions list, or disease episode screen for 'John Zenon' showing an 'H90.x' code (e.g., H90.3) or 'sensorineural hearing loss' as an active condition.

task_completion.description: Verify a new clinical evaluation was recorded today for John Zenon capturing Systolic BP, Diastolic BP, and Heart Rate.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a clinical evaluation or patient encounter screen for 'John Zenon' dated today. The form must have values entered for Systolic Blood Pressure, Diastolic Blood Pressure, and Heart Rate (bpm).

task_completion.description: Verify at least 2 metabolic screening lab tests were ordered for John Zenon.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a laboratory test order screen or list for 'John Zenon' showing at least two distinct lab tests ordered (e.g., Fasting Glucose, HbA1c, CBC, CMP, or similar metabolic screenings).

task_completion.description: Verify a follow-up appointment was scheduled for John Zenon 21 to 30 days from today.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an appointment booking screen, list, or calendar showing a scheduled appointment for 'John Zenon'. The date of the appointment must be between 21 and 30 days after the date of the clinical evaluation.

integrity.description: The agent used the GNU Health GUI client to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GNU Health desktop application interface being actively used, rather than terminal commands or direct database manipulation.

integrity.description: The agent entered the data into the actual GNU Health system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data (conditions, evaluations, lab orders, appointments) should appear within the standard GNU Health form fields, lists, or dialog boxes, not in a text editor or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script or terminal to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running python scripts, API calls, or SQL queries to insert the records directly into the health50 database.
